Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows evidence of impaired renal function (elevated creatinine, proteinuria, low serum protein, and albumin in urine), glycosuria and ketonuria despite low blood glucose, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). Elevated alkaline phosphatase may suggest possible hepatic or bone involvement. Findings are consistent with nephrotic syndrome and possible acute kidney injury; further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.
